steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: On the left of the blank we have the fragment “was a” which needs a noun to complete the subject‑verb‑complement pattern. On the right we see “the explosion…” beginning with a definite article, signaling a noun phrase that renames or explains the noun just given.

steps.title: Apply the dash rule
steps.content: An em dash sets off an appositive phrase, so “supernova—the” (choice C) correctly links the noun to its explanation. The other choices misuse punctuation: a comma creates a comma splice, a period leaves a fragment, and a colon incorrectly introduces a list. That matches choice C — C is correct.
